publicationStmt.p: Transcribed from Bodleian Library, Lovelace Byron collection, Box 165, folios 162-164. Transcription by Lisa Gold (2022). TEI conversion for Sextant pipeline, 2026.

div.p: St James' Sqre Weddy. Night

Dearest Mate. My journey was most successful in all respects. I arrived at Bridgwater Station full three quarters of an hour before the time;-- which I did not at all mind, & much preferred to running it near the other way. Harmes tells me the children but just saved it; the departure bell ringing as she was paying for the tickets. What
a narrow escape!
I apprehended as much; & you may remember how I urged them getting off quite early.--
I arrived in Town before a 1/4 past five !--
The Children were at by 1/2 past six. Harmes reached the Square at 1/2 past seven.--
At Reading Station I heard it announced that one of the poor wretches in the hospital had just died. A fellow- -passenger, who appeared to be an intelligent man, told me the accident was an
unavoidable one; owing to its being dark, & in a locality where no one could have anticipated such a land-slip. He pointed out to me the place. They were still repairing it.--
I thought the weather quite perfect for a journey today.
I send you a letter I found from the Hen; & I hope to enclose you Miss B--'s tomorrow.--
Ralph does not seem over-fatigued. But he enjoyed some nice buns I
had bought for him in Town, (& presented him with on his arrival) very much; as well as a capital mutton-chop; for his supper.--
I hear that Byron complained terribly of its being a very long day & too much Railway; & that Annabella was suffering sadly with a corn, for which I pity her but a little, poor thing!--
Pray tell the maids to bring with them to Ockham my very small feather cushion, which has a spotted cover. It has been forgotten.-- Please remember this.--
Thursday Morning:
The children seem to have reached Ockham later last night than I can account for. Miss B-- was to write immediately on arriving.--
Harmes is to go down today. She is just returned from Dr L--, who she says is entirely satisfied about Ralph; with proper treatment. But I am very glad I brought him.
I am myself expecting Dr L-- this morning; finding it necessary not to delay consulting him. I am growing larger now quite by inches; especially in the neck. And
the distress in breathing; necessity for enormous doses, &c; seem to get much worse.
But for my own determination, & provision of constant occupations & little excitements; I should be just as ever.--
What has come to me, I cannot imagine!--

Pray forward to me all my letters. I ought (as you will see by the enclosed) to have had this morning one from Hester, which I conclude arrived at Ashley yesterday after I was gone, (or ought so to have arrived).
There seems to have been much wet in the night here. And yesterday I found everything seeming ditto.
[No closing. Postscript appears on page 1 but is transcribed here as page 7]
P.S. Thursdy. 4 o'clock
Mr Lauzelle appoints you at 1/2 past 12 o'clock on Thursday the 6th. And he sends you by post today a small bottle of black stuff to apply.
